QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_39

question.text: The ETCD (Exchange Traded Commodity Derivatives) schemes must publish their NAV by what time?

answer.text: 3 PM on the same business day (T-day)
9 AM on the next business day (T+1)
11 PM on the same business day (T-day)
10 AM on the next business day (T+1)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ETCD schemes have a NAV publication deadline of 9 AM on T+1 (next business day). This is because commodity markets have extended trading hours and the final settlement prices may be available late. **The Trap:** This is different from both the standard 11 PM T-day deadline and the FoF 10 AM T+1 deadline. ETCD has its own unique 9 AM T+1 timeline. **Cross-Exam Context:** Different scheme types have different NAV timelines: most schemes 11 PM T-day, FoF/overseas 10 AM T+1, ETCD 9 AM T+1. This is a popular exam question across NISM exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you deal with commodity-linked funds, inform clients that NAV will be available only the next morning. This is normal for ETCD schemes and not a sign of any operational delay.
